design_philosophy: Swiss & High-Contrast meets Old Money Tech. A B2B real-estate and tech fusion focused on operational clarity. Linear/Stripe inspired minimalism with rigorous bento-grid structures, zero lifestyle fluff, and high-fidelity CSS-based system mockups.

headings.rules: Never center by default. Left-aligned for clear structural hierarchy. Use tracking-tighter for massive hero text.

body.rules: Use for all functional text, descriptions, and data points.

mono.rules: Use for overlines, labels, and system status indicators.

surfaces.name: The Grid Canvas
surfaces.usage: Use as a subtle background pattern for the Hero and FAQ sections to enforce the 'system/operational' feel.

surfaces.name: Bento Cards
surfaces.classes: bg-white border border-slate-200 shadow-sm rounded-2xl overflow-hidden hover:shadow-md transition-shadow duration-300
surfaces.usage: Use for 'Impact', 'Solution', and 'How it works' grid items.

patterns.hero: 2-column layout on desktop: Left side massive typography and CTA, Right side isometric or floating CSS-based dashboard mockup.
patterns.bento_grid: Asymmetric grid (e.g., 1x large horizontal card, 2x vertical cards) to map 'Solution' features.
patterns.faq: Narrow single-column centered layout (max-w-3xl) for high readability.

rules: Use subtle fade-up animations for text block reveals (`y: 20, opacity: 0` to `y: 0, opacity: 1`).
Add micro-interactions to buttons (`whileHover={{ scale: 1.02 }} whileTap={{ scale: 0.98 }}`).
No wild bouncing or spinning. Keep it clinical and professional.

media.strategy: NO LIFESTYLE STOCK PHOTOS. Zero. Use CSS, SVG, and Phosphor icons to build abstract 'dashboard mockups' (skeletons, charts, metrics, data tables) that look like a highly advanced coliving management OS.

components.navigation: Sticky, glassmorphic top bar with Logo (text 'BaseLayer') on left, CTA ('Book Demo') on right.
components.hero: H1: 'Reduce friction. Control cost per unit.' Subtext: 'A simple operational layer between you and your residents.' CTA: 'Book a Demo'. Right visual: Abstract UI mockup of a resident coordination dashboard built with HTML/Tailwind.
components.problem_section: Bento grid detailing 'Small frictions add up' - maintenance requests, lost keys, WhatsApp chaos.
components.impact_section: High-contrast dark section (bg-slate-900 text-white) to emphasize business impact ('And they directly impact your business').
components.solution_section: Feature grid showing the platform capabilities.
components.how_it_works: 4-step vertical timeline or horizontal cards showing the operator process.
components.calendly_cta: A dedicated section at the bottom embedding Calendly via iframe (`https://calendly.com/leon-boulliung-grabitautomaten/leon-call`) wrapped in a clean container.

instructions_to_main_agent: You are building a B2B SaaS landing page in React using pure JS (.js/.jsx), NOT TypeScript. Do not create .tsx files.
Install and use 'framer-motion' for scroll reveals and layout animations.
Install and use '@phosphor-icons/react' for all iconography.
Do not use external images for mockups. Build a 'fake dashboard' using div blocks, borders, subtle shadows, and skeleton-like loaders to represent the BaseLayer app in the Hero section.
Embed the Calendly link `https://calendly.com/leon-boulliung-grabitautomaten/leon-call` in the final CTA section using an iframe or react-calendly.
Use standard Tailwind CSS classes. Extend the tailwind config with the fonts (Chivo and Inter) and custom colors.
Include 'data-testid' on all interactive elements (buttons, form inputs, accordion triggers).
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Always use JS (.js or .jsx) instead of TSX as requested by the user.
Avoid generic centered layouts except for the FAQ section.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy and soft box-shadows.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
DO NOT apply universal transition (`transition: all`). Add transitions for specific interactive elements.
DO NOT center align the app container. No `.App { text-align: center; }`.
Use modern pill-shaped or sharp buttons with interaction animations.
NEVER use emojis for icons. Use the specified icon library (@phosphor-icons/react).
Add generous spacing (py-24 or py-32) to let the sections breathe.
DO NOT use a transparent background in the header if content scrolls under it; use glassmorphism.
